title: Siege / Hostile-Takeover Contest State
description: Authoritative record of ONE org-vs-org hostile-takeover contest against a territory claim (or a player-city node). This is the concrete state machine for the MUSH's planned-but-undelivered 'Drop 6D' (Guide_11 §7): contest state machine, timer, rival-org no-consent PvP window, hostile takeover. The headline PvP loop. Server owns it; survives restart; the slow Director/territory tick advances its timers. See FACTION_TERRITORY_DESIGN for the full state diagram and rules.

source.source_policy: Original design for Drop 6D, which the MUSH specced as 'Planned' but never delivered. Grounded in Guide_11's claim/influence model and the security-zone consent model; numbers are starting points, owner-tunable. Clone Wars era. Server-authoritative: all RNG/timers owned by the server.

claim_id.description: The territory_claim under contest. (For a city node, the city's claim id.)

zone_id.description: Must be contested or lawless. Sieges cannot be declared on secured zones or on city-upgraded-to-secured citizen rooms.

attacker_org_id.description: Challenging org. Must meet a min-influence-in-zone gate to declare (default Foothold/50).

state.description: Current state-machine state. Transitions and dwell-times are defined in FACTION_TERRITORY_DESIGN §Siege State Machine. Terminal states: captured, repelled, aborted.

phase_started_unix.description: Wall-clock epoch seconds the current state was entered. Timer math is relative to this.

phase_deadline_unix.description: Epoch seconds at which the current state auto-advances or auto-resolves if conditions are unmet.

config.description: Snapshot of the timing/threshold config this siege runs under (captured at declaration so a live siege is not disturbed by an admin retune). All values owner-tunable; defaults shown are starting points, NOT a flagged owner decision baked in.

warning_hours.description: DECLARED dwell: defender-warning window before the contest window opens. The 'planned-but-watched' telegraph (mirrors Guide_12's 24h city-expansion telegraph philosophy).

contest_window_hours.description: ACTIVE dwell: the no-consent PvP + control-point capture window.

lockout_hours.description: Cooldown after a resolved siege before the same node can be contested again (default 7 days).

attacker_min_influence.description: Attacker's required org influence in the zone to declare (Foothold).

control_threshold.description: Fraction of accumulated contest score the attacker must hold at window close to capture. Below it = repelled.

pvp_consent.description: While state == active, members of attacker_org and defender_org are mutually flagged for NO-CONSENT PvP at the contested node and its adjacency, regardless of the zone's normal consent rules. This is the rival-org no-consent PvP window. Outside this window/scope, normal security-zone consent applies. Transient flag; the server re-derives it from state each tick and never trusts a client.

scope_node_ids.description: Nodes where the no-consent window applies (the claim node plus declared adjacency).

score.description: Accumulated contest score. The server owns all of this (server-authoritative; ties into action-window combat outcomes). Capture is decided by attacker_fraction vs config.control_threshold at window close.

contributions.description: Audit log of scoring events that moved the contest (PvP kills in scope, control-point hold ticks, guard NPC defeats, sabotage objectives). Drives 'did you see what happened' social currency and post-siege news.

outcome.description: Set when the siege reaches a terminal state. Captured => claim ownership transfers to attacker_org (income/guard reset, lockout begins). Repelled => defender keeps the claim, lockout begins. Aborted => declaration cancelled before ACTIVE (e.g. attacker influence fell below gate).

news_headline.description: Player-facing news line (feeds the Director news bulletin).

declared_unix.description: Epoch seconds the siege was declared.
